ability.en: the mental or physical power or skill needed to do something

ex.en: She has the ability to solve problems quickly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 문제를 빠르게 해결하는 능력이 있다.

abroad.en: in or to a foreign country

ex.en: She studied abroad for two years.

accept.en: agree to take or receive something that is offered

ex.en: She accepted his invitation to the party.

accept.en: believe that something is true or must be dealt with

ex.en: He finally accepted that he was wrong.
ex.ko: 그는 마침내 자신이 틀렸다는 것을 받아들였다.

accept.en: allow someone to join a school, group, or organization

ex.en: She was accepted into the university's law program.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 대학교 법학 과정에 합격했다.

acceptable.en: good enough to be allowed or approved of

ex.en: His work was acceptable, but not excellent.
ex.ko: 그의 작업은 받아들일 만했지만, 뛰어나지는 않았다.

accident.en: an unexpected event that causes damage or injury

ex.en: He was injured in a car accident.

accident.en: something that happens by chance, without being planned

ex.en: I met her by accident at the mall.

account.en: be the explanation or reason for something, or make up a stated amount or part of something

ex.en: Rent accounts for half of my monthly spending.
ex.ko: 집세가 내 한 달 지출의 절반을 차지한다.

account.en: an arrangement to keep money at a bank, or a personal profile on a website

ex.en: I opened a bank account last week.

account.en: a spoken or written report of an event

ex.en: He gave a detailed account of the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사고에 대해 자세히 이야기했다.

achieve.en: succeed in reaching a goal, usually after effort

ex.en: She achieved her goal of running a marathon.

across.en: from one side of something to the other

ex.en: They swam across the lake.

across.en: on or to the opposite side

ex.en: The bank is just across from my house.
ex.ko: 은행은 우리 집 바로 건너편에 있다.

act.en: do something, or behave in a particular way

ex.en: The children acted like adults.

act.en: perform a role in a play, movie, or TV show

ex.en: She acts in a popular TV drama.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인기 있는 TV 드라마에서 연기한다.

act.en: something that someone does; a deed

ex.en: It was a kind act to help the old man.
ex.ko: 그 노인을 도운 것은 친절한 행동이었다.

act.en: one of the main parts into which a play is divided

ex.en: The play has three acts.

actually.en: used to say what is really true, especially when it is surprising

ex.en: I thought she was angry, but actually she wasn't.
ex.ko: 그녀가 화난 줄 알았는데, 사실은 아니었다.

addition.en: the process of adding numbers together

ex.en: Children learn addition in first grade.

addition.en: a thing that is added to something else

ex.en: The new pool is a great addition to the hotel.
ex.ko: 새 수영장은 그 호텔에 훌륭한 추가 시설이다.

additional.en: more than what already exists; extra

ex.en: We need additional information before deciding.

adjective.en: a word that describes a noun

ex.en: 'Big' and 'happy' are adjectives.

adjust.en: change something slightly to make it more correct or suitable

ex.en: He adjusted the volume on the TV.

adjust.en: get used to a new situation or condition

ex.en: It took her a while to adjust to the new school.
ex.ko: 그녀가 새 학교에 적응하는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

admire.en: respect or feel great approval for someone or something

ex.en: I admire her courage.

admire.en: look at something with pleasure and appreciation

ex.en: They stopped to admire the view.

admit.en: agree, often unwillingly, that something is true

ex.en: He admitted that he was wrong.

admit.en: allow someone to enter a place or join an organization

ex.en: She was admitted to a top university.
ex.ko: 그녀는 명문 대학교에 입학 허가를 받았다.

adult.en: a person who is fully grown

ex.en: Adults must pay full price for tickets.
An adult elephant can weigh six tons.
ex.ko: 다 자란 코끼리는 6톤까지 나갈 수 있다.

advanced.en: having reached a high level of skill, knowledge, or difficulty

ex.en: She's taking an advanced English class.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고급 영어 수업을 듣고 있다.

ex.en: Japan has advanced technology.

advantage.en: something that helps you or puts you in a better position than others

ex.en: Speaking two languages gives her an advantage in the job market.
ex.ko: 두 언어를 구사하는 것은 그녀에게 취업 시장에서 이점이 된다.

adventure.en: an exciting or dangerous experience or activity

ex.en: They went on an adventure in the mountains.

advertisement.en: a public notice that promotes a product, service, or event

ex.en: I saw an advertisement for a new phone.

advertising.en: the business or activity of making advertisements

ex.en: He works in advertising.

advice.en: an opinion or suggestion about what someone should do

ex.en: She gave me good advice about my career.
ex.ko: 그녀는 내 진로에 대해 좋은 조언을 해주었다.

advise.en: give someone advice about what they should do

ex.en: The doctor advised him to rest.

affair.en: a matter or event that concerns someone

ex.en: Foreign affairs are handled by the government.

affair.en: a secret romantic relationship, especially when one person is already married

ex.en: He had an affair with his colleague.

against.en: in opposition to; not agreeing with

ex.en: Many people spoke against the new law.
ex.ko: 많은 사람들이 새 법에 반대하여 말했다.

against.en: touching or leaning on something, or hitting something

ex.en: She leaned against the wall.
She takes care of her aged parents.
The camp is for children aged 10 to 12.

agency.en: a business that provides a particular service on behalf of others

ex.en: She booked the trip through a travel agency.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행사를 통해 그 여행을 예약했다.

ex.en: The agency is responsible for protecting the environment.

agent.en: a person who acts on behalf of another person or organization

ex.en: He bought the house through a real estate agent.
ex.ko: 그는 부동산 중개인을 통해 그 집을 샀다.

agent.en: a person who works for a government organization, especially collecting secret information

ex.en: The FBI agent investigated the case.

ahead.en: in front, or forward in space

ex.en: I can see the mountains ahead.

ahead.en: before a particular time, or in the future

ex.en: We should plan ahead for the trip.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 여행을 미리 계획해야 한다.

ahead.en: in a leading position in a race or competition

ex.en: Our team is ahead by ten points.

air.en: the mixture of gases that surrounds the Earth and that we breathe

ex.en: Open the window for some fresh air.

air.en: the space above the ground; the sky

ex.en: The bird flew high in the air.

aisle.en: a long narrow passage between rows of seats or shelves

ex.en: She walked down the aisle of the supermarket.

alarm.en: make someone feel frightened or worried about possible danger

ex.en: The news alarmed the whole town.
ex.ko: 그 소식은 온 마을을 불안하게 만들었다.

alarm.en: a sound or device that warns people of danger

ex.en: The fire alarm went off during class.

alarm clock.en: a clock that can be set to make a sound at a particular time to wake someone up

ex.en: I set my alarm clock for 7 a.m.
The plant is still alive after the winter.
ex.ko: 그 식물은 겨울이 지나고도 여전히 살아있다.

alive.en: full of energy, activity, or excitement

ex.en: The city comes alive at night.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 밤이 되면 활기를 띤다.

allow.en: let someone do something, or let something happen

ex.en: My parents don't allow me to stay out late.
ex.ko: 부모님은 내가 늦게까지 밖에 있는 것을 허락하지 않으신다.

alright.en: acceptable or satisfactory (informal spelling of 'all right')

ex.en: Is it alright if I leave early today?

although.en: despite the fact that; even though

ex.en: Although it was raining, they went for a walk.
ex.ko: 비가 오고 있었지만, 그들은 산책을 나갔다.

ambition.en: a strong desire to achieve something, such as success or power

ex.en: Her ambition is to become a doctor.

among.en: in the middle of, or included in, a group of three or more

ex.en: She was among the winners.

amused.en: feeling happy or entertained because something is funny or enjoyable

ex.en: The children were amused by the clown's tricks.

amusement.en: a feeling of enjoyment or fun, or something that provides this

ex.en: To our amusement, the dog started dancing.
ex.ko: 우리가 재미있어 하게도, 그 개는 춤을 추기 시작했다.

ancestor.en: a person from whom you are descended, especially one more distant than a grandparent

ex.en: Her ancestors came from Ireland.

ancient.en: belonging to a time long ago in history

ex.en: They visited ancient ruins in Greece.

angel.en: a spiritual being believed to be a messenger of God, often shown with wings

ex.en: The children dressed up as angels for the play.
ex.ko: 아이들은 연극을 위해 천사 옷을 입었다.

ex.en: He slammed the door angrily.
ex.ko: 그는 화가 나서 문을 쾅 닫았다.

ankle.en: the joint connecting the foot to the leg

ex.en: She twisted her ankle while running.

anniversary.en: the date each year on which a special event happened in a previous year

ex.en: They celebrated their wedding anniversary.

annoy.en: make someone feel slightly angry or irritated

ex.en: His constant complaining annoys me.
ex.ko: 그의 끊임없는 불평은 나를 짜증나게 한다.

ex.en: It's annoying when people talk during a movie.
ex.ko: 영화 볼 때 사람들이 이야기하는 것은 짜증난다.

anxious.en: feeling worried or nervous about something that might happen

ex.en: She felt anxious before the exam.

anxious.en: eager to do something very much

ex.en: He was anxious to start his new job.
ex.ko: 그는 새 직장을 몹시 시작하고 싶어했다.

anymore.en: any longer; from now on (used in negative sentences)

ex.en: I don't live there anymore.
ex.ko: 나는 더 이상 거기 살지 않는다.

anyway.en: used to say that something is true or will happen despite other things

ex.en: It was raining, but we went out anyway.

anywhere.en: in, at, or to any place

ex.en: You can sit anywhere you like.

apart.en: separated by a distance, or into pieces

ex.en: The two houses are only a mile apart.
ex.ko: 그 두 집은 겨우 1마일 떨어져 있다.

ex.en: Apart from a few mistakes, the report was excellent.
ex.ko: 몇 가지 실수를 제외하면, 그 보고서는 훌륭했다.

apartment.en: a set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building

ex.en: She lives in a small apartment downtown.

apologize.en: say sorry for doing something wrong

ex.en: He apologized for being late.

apparently.en: based on what you have heard or read, though not completely certain

ex.en: Apparently, the flight was delayed by two hours.
ex.ko: 보아하니 그 비행기는 두 시간 지연된 것 같다.

ex.en: A rainbow appeared after the rain.

appear.en: seem to be a particular thing or have a particular quality

ex.en: She appears to be very happy.

appearance.en: the way someone or something looks

ex.en: She is very confident about her appearance.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 외모에 대해 매우 자신감이 있다.

appearance.en: the act of coming into sight or becoming public

ex.en: The singer made a surprise appearance at the party.

apply.en: make a formal request for a job, place at a school, etc.

ex.en: She applied for a job at the bank.

apply.en: be relevant to a situation, or make use of a rule or method

ex.en: This rule applies to everyone.

appointment.en: an arrangement to meet someone or be somewhere at a particular time

ex.en: I have a dentist appointment tomorrow.
I really appreciate your help.

appreciate.en: understand the value or importance of something

ex.en: You don't appreciate how hard this job is.
ex.ko: 너는 이 일이 얼마나 힘든지 제대로 알지 못한다.

appropriate.en: suitable or right for a particular situation

ex.en: Please wear appropriate clothing for the interview.

architecture.en: the art and science of designing and constructing buildings

ex.en: She studied architecture at university.

area.en: a particular part of a place, town, or country

ex.en: They live in a quiet area of the city.

area.en: a subject or field of activity or study

ex.en: Math is her area of expertise.

argue.en: have an angry disagreement with someone

ex.en: They argued about money.

argue.en: give reasons to support an opinion

ex.en: She argued that the plan was too risky.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 계획이 너무 위험하다고 주장했다.

ex.en: They had an argument about the bill.

argument.en: a set of reasons given to support an opinion

ex.en: His argument for the new policy was convincing.
ex.ko: 새 정책에 대한 그의 주장은 설득력이 있었다.

armchair.en: a comfortable chair with parts on each side to support your arms

ex.en: He fell asleep in his armchair.
Armed police guarded the building.

artificial.en: made by people, not occurring naturally

ex.en: The room was lit by artificial light.
The baby fell asleep in the car.

assistant.en: a person who helps someone else in their work

ex.en: She works as a shop assistant.

association.en: an organized group of people with a shared purpose

ex.en: He is a member of the local sports association.

association.en: a connection in your mind between different ideas or memories

ex.en: The song brings back happy associations for her.
ex.ko: 그 노래는 그녀에게 행복한 기억을 떠올리게 한다.

astronaut.en: a person trained to travel and work in a spacecraft

ex.en: The astronaut spent six months on the space station.

athlete.en: a person who is trained in or good at sports

ex.en: The athlete trained every day for the Olympics.
ex.ko: 그 운동선수는 올림픽을 위해 매일 훈련했다.

atom.en: the smallest unit of a chemical element

ex.en: Everything is made up of atoms.

attack.en: use violence to try to hurt or defeat someone

ex.en: The dog attacked the mailman.

attack.en: an act of using violence to try to hurt or defeat someone

ex.en: The city suffered a surprise attack.

attack.en: a sudden occurrence of an illness

ex.en: He had a heart attack last year.

attempt.en: make an effort to do something, especially something difficult

ex.en: She attempted to fix the computer herself.

attempt.en: an effort to do something, especially something difficult

ex.en: His first attempt at the exam failed.
ex.ko: 그의 첫 번째 시험 시도는 실패했다.

attention.en: the act of watching, listening to, or thinking about something carefully

ex.en: Please pay attention to the teacher.

attitude.en: the way someone thinks or feels about something, shown in their behavior

ex.en: She has a positive attitude toward life.
ex.ko: 그녀는 삶에 대해 긍정적인 태도를 가지고 있다.

ex.en: He has an attractive smile.

audience.en: the group of people watching or listening to a performance, speech, etc.

ex.en: The audience clapped loudly after the concert.
ex.ko: 관객들은 콘서트가 끝난 후 크게 박수를 쳤다.

audio.en: relating to sound, especially recorded sound

ex.en: The video has poor audio quality.

author.en: a person who writes a book, article, or other written work

ex.en: She is the author of several bestselling novels.

automatic.en: operating by itself, without needing a person to control it

ex.en: The doors are automatic.

automatically.en: without needing a person to operate or control it

ex.en: The lights turn on automatically at night.

average.en: the usual or typical amount, calculated by adding several numbers and dividing by how many there are

ex.en: The average temperature in July is 30 degrees.
He is an average student.

avoid.en: stay away from someone or something, or prevent something from happening

ex.en: She avoided eye contact during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의 중에 눈을 마주치는 것을 피했다.

award.en: a prize given for an achievement

ex.en: She won an award for her research.

award.en: give someone a prize or right officially

ex.en: The city awarded him a medal for bravery.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 그에게 용기에 대한 상을 수여했다.

ex.en: The weather was awful all weekend.

background.en: the part of a picture or scene that is behind the main objects

ex.en: The mountains form a beautiful background in the photo.
ex.ko: 그 사진에서 산이 아름다운 배경을 이룬다.

background.en: a person's education, family, and experience

ex.en: She has a background in marketing.

badly.en: in a poor or unsatisfactory way, or to a great degree

ex.en: He was badly injured in the accident.

badminton.en: a sport in which players hit a shuttlecock over a net with rackets

ex.en: We played badminton in the park.

bake.en: cook food, especially bread or cakes, using dry heat in an oven

ex.en: She baked cookies for the party.

balcony.en: a platform on the outside of a building, connected to a room

ex.en: They had coffee on the balcony.

balloon.en: a small rubber bag that you blow air into for decoration or fun

ex.en: The kids decorated the room with balloons.

banking.en: the business of keeping and lending money, done by banks

ex.en: He works in banking.

barbecue.en: a meal cooked outdoors over an open fire, or the event itself

ex.en: We had a barbecue in the backyard.

barbecue.en: cook food outdoors over an open fire

ex.en: He barbecued some chicken for dinner.

barber.en: a person whose job is to cut men's hair

ex.en: He went to the barber for a haircut.

bargain.en: something bought for a good price, cheaper than usual

ex.en: This coat was a real bargain at half price.
ex.ko: 이 코트는 반값이라 정말 싸게 산 물건이었다.

bargain.en: discuss the price or conditions of something with someone

ex.en: He bargained with the seller over the price.

base.en: use something as the foundation or starting point for an idea or plan

ex.en: The movie is based on a true story.

base.en: the lowest or supporting part of something

ex.en: The base of the lamp is made of metal.

base.en: a place used as a center of operations, especially by the military

ex.en: The soldiers returned to their base.

basic.en: forming the necessary starting point or most important part of something

ex.en: Everyone should learn basic first aid.

basically.en: in the most essential or fundamental way

ex.en: Basically, we need more money.

basket.en: a container made of woven material, used for carrying or holding things

ex.en: She put the apples in a basket.

batch.en: a group or quantity of things made or dealt with at one time

ex.en: She baked a batch of cookies.

battery.en: a device that stores and produces electricity to power devices

ex.en: I need to buy new batteries for the remote.
ex.ko: 리모컨에 넣을 새 건전지를 사야 한다.

bay.cue: 바다가 육지 쪽으로 굽어 들어온 곳
bay.en: a part of the sea or a lake that curves into the land

ex.en: They anchored the boat in the bay.

beauty.en: the quality of being pleasing to look at

ex.en: The beauty of the sunset amazed everyone.
ex.ko: 그 노을의 아름다움은 모두를 감탄하게 했다.

ex.en: She was known as the town beauty.

been.en: the past participle of 'be', used to form perfect tenses

ex.en: I have been to Paris twice.
ex.ko: 나는 파리에 두 번 가본 적이 있다.

beer.en: an alcoholic drink made from grain

ex.en: He ordered a cold beer with his meal.
ex.ko: 그는 식사와 함께 시원한 맥주를 주문했다.

beg.en: ask for something in an urgent or emotional way

ex.en: She begged him not to leave.

beg.en: ask people for money or food because you are poor

ex.en: The homeless man begged for food on the street.

beginner.en: a person who is just starting to learn or do something

ex.en: This yoga class is for beginners.
ex.ko: 이 요가 수업은 초보자를 위한 것이다.

beginning.en: the point in time or space at which something starts

ex.en: The story starts at the beginning of summer.
ex.ko: 그 이야기는 여름이 시작될 때 시작된다.

behavior.en: the way a person or animal acts

ex.en: The teacher praised the students' good behavior.

being.en: a living creature, or the state of existing

ex.en: Human beings need food, water, and sleep.

belly.en: the front part of the body between the chest and the legs; the stomach area

ex.en: The dog rolled over to have its belly rubbed.
ex.ko: 그 개는 배를 문질러 달라고 뒤집었다.

belong.en: be a member of a group, or be owned by someone

ex.en: She belongs to the school swimming team.

belong.en: be in the right or proper place

ex.en: This book belongs on the top shelf.
ex.ko: 이 책은 맨 위 선반에 있어야 한다.

belt.en: a strip of leather or cloth worn around the waist to hold up clothing or for decoration

ex.en: He wore a brown leather belt.

bench.en: a long seat for several people, often found outdoors

ex.en: They sat on a bench in the park.

bend.en: move part of your body, or make something curved, at an angle

ex.en: She bent down to tie her shoes.

bend.en: a curved part of a road, river, or path

ex.en: Slow down before the sharp bend in the road.

besides.en: in addition to what has already been said; also

ex.en: I don't want to go. Besides, it's too late.
ex.ko: 나는 가고 싶지 않아. 게다가, 너무 늦었어.

ex.en: Besides English, she speaks French and German.
ex.ko: 영어 외에도, 그녀는 프랑스어와 독일어를 한다.

beyond.en: on or to the further side of something; further than

ex.en: The village lies beyond the mountains.

bill.en: a piece of paper showing how much money you owe for goods or services

ex.en: Can we have the bill, please?
He paid with a twenty-dollar bill.

bill.en: a proposed law discussed by a government

ex.en: The senate passed the new bill.

bit.en: a small amount or piece of something

ex.en: Can I have a bit more coffee?

bite.en: use your teeth to cut into or through something

ex.en: The dog bit the mailman's leg.

bite.en: a small amount of food taken into the mouth at one time

ex.en: She took a bite of the sandwich.
ex.ko: 그녀는 샌드위치를 한 입 베어 물었다.

blackboard.en: a dark board that teachers write on with chalk

ex.en: The teacher wrote the homework on the blackboard.

blame.en: say or think that someone or something is responsible for a mistake or problem

ex.en: Don't blame me for your mistake.

blame.en: responsibility for a mistake or problem

ex.en: He took the blame for the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사고에 대한 책임을 졌다.

blanket.en: a large piece of thick cloth used to keep warm, especially in bed

ex.en: She wrapped the baby in a warm blanket.
Her sister has blonde hair.
She is a natural blonde.

blood.en: the red liquid that flows through the body

ex.en: She donated blood at the hospital.
The roses bloom in early summer.

bloom.en: a flower, or the state of flowering

ex.en: The garden was in full bloom.

board game.en: a game played by moving pieces on a specially designed board

ex.en: We played a board game on Friday night.

boil.en: heat a liquid until it bubbles and turns to steam, or cook food this way

ex.en: Boil the water before adding the pasta.
She had a boiled egg for breakfast.

bonus.en: extra money or something else given in addition to what is expected

ex.en: He received a bonus for his hard work.
ex.ko: 그는 열심히 일한 대가로 보너스를 받았다.

bookcase.en: a piece of furniture with shelves for storing books

ex.en: He put the new books on the bookcase.

bookshelf.en: a shelf on which to keep books

ex.en: The bookshelf was full of novels.

bookshop.en: a shop where books are sold

ex.en: She bought a novel at the bookshop.

bored.en: feeling tired and impatient because something is not interesting

ex.en: The students looked bored during the long lecture.
ex.ko: 학생들은 긴 강의 동안 지루해 보였다.

boss.en: the person who is in charge of you at work

ex.en: My boss gave me a new project today.
ex.ko: 내 상사는 오늘 나에게 새 프로젝트를 주었다.

bother.en: annoy someone, or take the trouble to do something

ex.en: Don't bother me while I'm working.
ex.ko: 내가 일하는 동안 귀찮게 하지 마.

bother.en: something that causes trouble or annoyance

ex.en: It's no bother to help you.
ex.ko: 너를 돕는 건 전혀 번거롭지 않아.

bra.en: an item of underwear worn by women to support the breasts

ex.en: She bought a new bra at the department store.

brainstorm.en: think of as many ideas as possible, often in a group

ex.en: The team brainstormed ideas for the new project.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 새 프로젝트를 위해 아이디어를 짜냈다.

ex.en: I had a brainstorm about how to solve the problem.
ex.ko: 나는 그 문제를 해결할 묘안이 떠올랐다.

brake.en: a device used to slow down or stop a vehicle

ex.en: He pressed the brake to stop the car.

brake.en: slow down or stop a vehicle by using the brake

ex.en: She braked suddenly to avoid the cat.

branch.en: a part of a tree that grows out from the trunk

ex.en: A bird landed on the branch.

branch.en: a local office or shop of a larger company or organization

ex.en: There's a bank branch near my house.
ex.ko: 우리 집 근처에 은행 지점이 있다.

brand.en: a particular product or company name that people recognize

ex.en: She only buys clothes from famous brands.

brave.en: having or showing courage in a difficult or dangerous situation

ex.en: The firefighter was brave to run into the burning building.
ex.ko: 그 소방관은 불타는 건물로 뛰어들 만큼 용감했다.

ex.en: A cool breeze blew through the open window.

bride.en: a woman on her wedding day

ex.en: The bride wore a beautiful white dress.
